title: SonarQube for IntelliJ Documentation
content:
# SonarQube for IDE Documentation

## [What is SonarQube for IDE?](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#what-is-sonarqube-for-ide "What is SonarQube for IDE?")

SonarQube for IDE (formerly known as SonarLint) is a free and open-source IDE plugin for automatic code review and static analysis brought to you by [Sonar](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarlint/ "Sonar"). It’s your first line of defense, designed to detect coding issues in real-time for [25 languages, frameworks, and IaC platforms](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/rules/ "25 languages, frameworks, and IaC platforms"). Your code is checked against an extensive set of rules that cover many attributes of code, such as maintainability, reliability, and security issues. It’s possible to analyze more rules, assign issues, share quality profiles, and more, with your team when running in connected mode with SonarQube (Server, Cloud) or SonarQube Community Build. See the [Connected mode benefits](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected mode benefits") list for more details.

Sonar’s IDE extensions are available for IntelliJ (and other JetBrains IDEs), Visual Studio, VS Code, and Eclipse, and can be installed directly from your IDE's plugin marketplace. SonarQube for IDE leverages over [5,000 language-specific Clean Code rules](https://rules.sonarsource.com/ "5,000 language-specific Clean Code rules").

## [The approach to Clean Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#clean-code "The approach to Clean Code")

[Clean Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/concepts/clean-code/introduction/ "Clean Code") is the standard for all code that results in secure, reliable, and maintainable software therefore, writing clean code is essential to maintaining a healthy codebase. This applies to all code: source code, test code, infrastructure as code, glue code, scripts, and more.

Sonar's [Clean as You Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/clean-as-you-code-in-the-ide/ "Clean as You Code") approach is a software development practice based on the principle that all new code (whether added or recently modified) should adhere to quality standards. SonarQube for IDE achieves this by providing automated code reviews that alert you to potential issues within your [new code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/investigating-issues/#focusing-on-new-code "new code"). This helps you maintain high standards and focus on code quality, ultimately leading to a healthier codebase over time.

[SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube/latest/instance-administration/quality-profiles/introduction/ "SonarQube Server"), [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/standards/managing-quality-profiles/introduction/ "SonarQube Cloud"), and [SonarQube Community Build](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-community-build/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-profiles/introduction/ "SonarQube Community Build") come with built-in quality profiles designed for each supported language, called the **Sonar Way** profile. The **Sonar way** activates a set of rules that should be applicable to most projects and is a starting point to help you implement clean code practices in your organization.

## [The Sonar solution](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#the-sonar-solution "The Sonar solution")

Sonar products are designed to help you achieve a state of Clean Code. By linking SonarQube for IDE with [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube/latest/user-guide/sonarlint-connected-mode/ "SonarQube Server"), [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/connected-mode/ "SonarQube Cloud"), or [SonarQube Community Build](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-community-build/user-guide/connected-mode/ "SonarQube Community Build"), the automated code analysis and reviews are performed at every stage of the development process; we call this the Sonar solution. This means your project settings, new code definitions, and quality profiles are applied locally to an analysis in the IDE. The Sonar solution is designed to help you achieve a state of Clean Code. Your project settings, new code definitions, and the quality profiles managed in SonarQube (Server, Cloud) or SonarQube Community Build are applied locally to an analysis in the IDE.

- SonarQube for IDE brings automated code reviews directly into your development environment, helping you catch issues as you write code. By providing immediate feedback, it enables engineers to identify and fix problems before they even commit, ensuring cleaner, higher-quality code from the start.
- Then, [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube/latest/analyzing-source-code/pull-request-analysis/ "SonarQube Server") and [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/pull-request-analysis/ "SonarQube Cloud") deliver powerful static code analysis by thoroughly reviewing each pull request before it’s merged. This proactive approach adds an essential layer of protection, ensuring code quality and preventing issues from entering your codebase.
- Finally, [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-gates/introduction-to-quality-gates/ "SonarQube Server"), [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/quality-gates/ "SonarQube Cloud"), and [SonarQube Community Build](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-community-build/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-gates/introduction-to-quality-gates/ "SonarQube Community Build") seamlessly integrate into your CI/CD pipeline, analyzing code on every build. By leveraging quality profiles and quality gates, they automatically block code with issues from being released to production, ensuring only maintainable, reliable, and secure code makes it through.

The Sonar solution helps you incorporate the Clean as You Code methodology by helping engineers pay attention to new code. [Focusing on writing new, clean code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/investigating-issues/#focusing-on-new-code "Focusing on writing new, clean code") during development ensures that all code released for production will be incrementally improved over time.

## [Connected Mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#connected-mode "Connected Mode")

Connected mode joins SonarQube for IDE with SonarQube (Server, Cloud) or SonarQube Community Build to deliver the full Sonar solution. SonarQube for IDE and SonarQube 9.9+, SonarQube Cloud, or SonarQube Community Build analyses help to ensure only clean code makes it into your project.

Be sure to check out all of the [Connected Mode benefits](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected Mode benefits").

## [Getting started](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#getting-started "Getting started")

Now that you've heard about how [SonarQube for IDE](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarlint/ "SonarQube for IDE") can help you write clean code, you are ready to try out SonarQube for IDE for yourself. After [installing SonarQube for Intelli](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/getting-started/installation/ "installing SonarQube for Intelli") for your IDE from the Marketplace, open a project using a [supported language](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/rules/ "supported language") and let it [run an analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/getting-started/running-an-analysis/ "run an analysis").

The [Investigating issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/investigating-issues/ "Investigating issues") explains how to find issues and focus on new code and each IDE explains how to [fix issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/fixing-issues/ "fix issues") in your code as you write.

## [Learn more](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#learn-more "Learn more")

Check out the entire suite of Sonar products: [SonarQube Server](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarqube/ "SonarQube Server"), [SonarQube Cloud](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarcloud/ "SonarQube Cloud"), and [SonarQube for IDE](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarlint/ "SonarQube for IDE").

Then, examine the types of issues that SonarQube for IDE detects when combined with SonarQube (Server, Cloud) and browse a full list of [Sonar Rules and Rule Descriptions](http://rules.sonarsource.com/ "Sonar Rules and Rule Descriptions") available for static code analysis.

## [Staying connected](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/#staying-connected "Staying connected")

Use the following links to follow SonarQube for IntelliJ behind the scenes:

- [Source code](https://github.com/SonarSource/sonarlint-intellij "Source code")
- [Issue tracker Jira](https://jira.sonarsource.com/ "Issue tracker Jira")

And if you need help, visit our [online community](https://community.sonarsource.com/c/sl/fault/6 "online community") to search for answers and reach out with questions!
